Commodity Code Tree Search (Added to Forms and Requests)
The commodity code tree makes it easier for users to identify the correct commodity code, ensuring more accurate spend reporting and application of commodity code rules.
Ability to Return a Document to a Previous Workflow Step
Provides the ability for an approver in a future step to return to the previous approver for any needed clarification, reduces the back and forth that may occur outside of Jaggaer.
Ability to add check-out rules on submission

Check out rules provide the ability to stop a requisition before it's submitted that can be tailored for specific business rules. The messages can be customized to provide users with specific instructions.
Ability to ‘Manage Supplier Profile History’ Access with Separate Permission

A configuration is available that allows for a new permission to specifically control access to the supplier profile history, supplier fulfillment center history, and supplier consolidated history pages.

1111 Compass Report – Sourcing+ Observations: • Hitachi Rail has higher solution adoption than other customers in the transportation and warehousing sector and is at a high performing level • Sourcing events are not awarded, which is below the industry benchmark and can drive compliance and reporting • Hitachi Rail is higher than the industry benchmark for ROI for their solution (both Soft and Hard aspects) • Estimated sourcing effectiveness is higher than the benchmark but average and can be further improved • Overall, it is estimated that Hitachi Rail is obtaining an 89.1x ROI on their sourcing solution High-level summary of metrics used: • Adoption Score – Calculation based on actual number of events and estimated avg. event size by Industry, ARR • Award % - Number of events awarded • Estimated Sourcing Effectiveness – Calculation based on actual number of events, estimated avg. event size by Industry and Sourcing Goal (based on % of Addressable spend) • ROI (Soft) – calculation based on process savings vs. ARR • ROI (Hard) – conservative estimation based on industry knowledge

1212 Sourcing+: Quarterly volume of ITT/PQQs Created 2022 – Q4 2024 Overall usage (creation of events) is trending positive YoY • 350 ITT/PQQs created in 2023 and 333 in 2024 (to end Nov) • - 35% ITT/PQQs created 2022 – 2023 • + 4% ITT/PQQs created 2023 – 2024 (predicted) Observations • Overall trend is a steady volume of events in 2024 with a small 4% increase predicted YoY over 2023

1313 Sourcing+ : ITT Status Split 2022 – Q4 2024 • ~ 2% of published ITTs awarded in the system in 2022 on average • However, in 2023/2024, this has dropped to 0%, Q: why is awarding not happening in the solution? Observations • 969 ITTs are in a “Final Evaluation” status, meaning they are not awarded in the platform • Look to increase awarding %, even when awarding is done offline to accurately record and report the decision in the platform and ensure compliance

1414 • Compliance – The only way to ensure that your organisation follows an approval process. This can include reducing maverick spend and ensuring that a certain percentage of the operating budget is spend on sourcing. Likewise, it facilitates internal and external audits • Automation and digitisation – To reduce the workload and improve efficiency you can bundle many of your needs and solutions in the Sourcing+ module. You won’t be needing a separate data storage, e-mail and IM communication, reminders. This way you have everything in one place Sourcing+ - Awarding Key Benefits of Awarding RFQs in JAGGAER • System Recommendations – As we move forward with AI in sourcing you can make the first steps by utilising advanced features like automatic awarding proposals and supplier statuses. You can expect a decrease in errors due to human interactions • Analytics and Reporting – If you need to report, externally or internally, on RFQ awarding you will be able to utilise reports within the platform, use Tableau for monitoring, reporting and analytics

1515 • Challenge – Suppliers are not able to collaborate with the system for a variety of reasons, but to ensure traceability, awarding should still be done within JAGGAER • Solution – Provide the ability to easily choose invited suppliers, registered suppliers and/or unregistered suppliers and provide their response into the platform • Value – Ensure compliance, further reporting and analysis, process clarity and transparency Sourcing+ - Awarding Offline Awarding Enhancements added in 23.1

1616 • An analysis tool to facilitate and visualise the evaluation of the RFQ. • The dashboards provide data in real-time and focus on the supplier’s commercial response • Answer the most common questions for the buyer • Gives new aspects that are interesting to consider • Compare item prices across suppliers • Please raise this with JAGGAER if you’d like to see this functionality turned on Sourcing+ - Awarding Advanced Scenario Analysis
